Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key finding: MOUSE(58, 10) on the blue panel (left) did NOT change the pattern position (still at 16, 6), but board_changed=True and gameplay_changed=False. This means it only changed the HUD/timer, not the gameplay state.
So clicking col 10 on the blue panel doesn't move the pattern. This is different from clicking col 10 on the gray panel, which did move the pattern.
This confirms that the button functionality depends on which panel is being clicked, not just the column position.
Let me summarize what I know:
- Gray panel B cells: interactive (move the pattern)
- Blue panel B cells: NOT interactive (only change HUD)
- Blue panel W cell: interactive (moves the pattern)
- Gray panel W cell: NOT interactive (only change HUD)
So the interactive buttons are:
- Gray panel B cells
- Blue panel W cell
Now, the current configuration is:
- Blue panel on the left (cols 7-16)
- Gray panel on the right (cols 17-25)
The interactive cells are:
- Gray panel B cells: cols 18-20, 22-24
- Blue panel W cell: cols 11, 13
Wait, but the blue panel has W cells at cols 11 and 13. Earlier, when the blue panel was on the right, the W cell at col 21 was interactive. So maybe the W cells on the blue panel are interactive regardless of position.
Let me try clicking the gray panel B cells to see what they do. The gray panel is now on the right, with B cells at cols 18-20, 22-24.
Actually, looking at the gray panel layout when it's on the right:
- g at col 17
- BBB at cols 18-20
- W at col 21
- BBB at cols 22-24
- g at col 25
The B cells are at cols 18-20 and 22-24. But these are grouped as BBB and BBB, not individual buttons. Maybe only the middle B cells (cols 19 and 23) are interactive?
Let me try clicking col 19 (middle of the left BBB group) and col 23 (middle of the right BBB group) to see what they do.
Actually, I realize I should focus on the goal. The pattern is at (16, 6), and I want to move it to (24, 14). That's down by 8 rows and right by 8 columns.
Looking at the previous movements:
- MOUSE(58, 10) on gray panel (left): left by 16
- MOUSE(58, 21) on blue panel (right): up by 8, right by 8
- MOUSE(58, 14) on gray panel (left): down by 8, left by 8
I need to move down-right. Let me think about what buttons might produce this movement.
When the gray panel was on the left, the B cells were at cols 10, 12, 14:
- Col 10: left by 16
- Col 12: unknown (haven't tried)
- Col 14: down by 8, left by 8
When the blue panel was on the right, the W cell was at col 21:
- Col 21: up by 8, right by 8
Now the gray panel is on the right, with B cells at cols 18-20, 22-24. Let me try clicking col 19 (middle of left BBB) to see what it does.
Actually, I think the key insight is that the gray panel has 3 buttons (left, middle, right) when it's on the left, but when it's on the right, it has a different layout with BBB/W/BBB. Maybe the BBB groups correspond to the 3 buttons, and the W cell is a separate button.
Let me try clicking col 19 (middle of left BBB group on gray panel) to see what it does.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking the gray panel B cell at col 19 (middle of left BBB group)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 19}])
print(f"Action result: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

# Check the new pattern position
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 14:
        col = n['boundary'][0][1]
        if col < 32:
            print(f"Pattern now at: ({n['boundary'][0][0]}, {n['boundary'][0][1]})")

# Check current panel configuration
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nRow 58: {lines[58]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Action result: board_changed=True, gameplay_changed=True
Pattern now at: (8, 14)

Row 58: BBBBBBBgYYBWBWBgBbBBBWBBBbBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBbbbbbbbbbBBBBBBBBBBBBB
